desc:
The river slows to a near halt as it widens, near the centre of the valley. On
the northern bank is a wooden pier overlooking the river, with a set of steps
winding up the steep riverbank to a house above. To the south a few crop fields
lie, interspersed with small huts where the elves store their farming tools.
Downstream the river flows underneath a slender bridge of obviously elven make,
and continues down the valley.
name: Peaceful Stretch in the River Bruinen

desc:
A graceful and thin bridge spans the river here, as the river speeds up somewhat
from its lazy pace upstream. To the west you can see the river become slightly
turbulent, while further downstream the rapids become rather larger as the
valley narrows. Swimming there would not be easy.
name: Under the Graceful Bridge

desc:
Formed of perfectly fitting stained oak planks, the pier provides a breathtaking
view of the river and valley. To the north steps ascend the riverbank, leading
up to the house. This is a popular place to hang out due to the moderate
temperature here, and is obviously used often judging by the many boats tied up
to the dock. A sign is shoved into the ground here.
name: Pier on the Bruinen

desc:
This quaint, spacious room has many windows offering a spectacular view of the
Bruinen River. There are many tables and chairs, and a fresh breeze seems to
always be blowing through. This looks like a nice place to relax for a while. A
sign is posted on the wall.
name: The River House

desc:
The talented baking staff of this shop makes fresh baked goods twice a day. The
smell of delicious cake, and fruit pastries makes your mouth water. You see a
sign on the display case.
name: The Bakery

desc:
Here the elves run a lively trade in supplies for adventurers. In addition to a
wide selection of items made here in Rivendell, this store often has something
exotic to offer. There is a sign posted behind the counter.
name: The General Store

desc:
The trail crosses the slope here, winding through the pine trees. Nearer the
valley floor the pines give way to oaks and beeches, perhaps because of the
shelter this deep ravine provides. Looking down you can see this path bridges
the river and heads onto the northern bank, meeting two other trails just to the
west of a large house. Above here the path climbs steeply to the top of the
slope, and leaves the valley.
name: Path through Some Trees

desc:
The path travels across the slope here, through a mixture of oak and beech
trees. Further down it leaves the trees and descends steeply to the valley
floor, where it meets another trail south of the river. To the east it levels
off for a while, before taking a final steep turn upwards and leaving the valley
altogether.
name: A Sloping Path

desc:
To the west and east the cliff is sheer and impassable. Even here the path has
to traverse across the slope, cutting into the rock face in parts. The track
itself is solidly built and looks completely safe, with rocks cleared away and
the sides shored up by wood at several points. Looking down you can see this
path follows the river east for a while, then bridges it and heads towards a
large house on the northern bank.
name: Steeply Sloping Path

desc:
The Bruinen cuts across the moors, widening from the gorge it is to the east and
west. Here it is a wide and deep valley, sheltered from the elements that make
the moorland so barren. Looking down you see verdant greenery, and some signs of
inhabitation: a large house lies on the northern bank of the river, surrounded
by cultivated fields and gardens. A well built and obvious path leads down the
valley wall, but does not continue into the moors in any noticeable form.
name: Top of a Steeply Sloping Path

desc:
The trail here follows the Bruinen on its southern bank. As the river flows
towards the end of the valley it accelerates and passes over some rapids,
gradually falling further below the level of the path. To the west, where the
riverbanks disappear in the gorge, the path can go no further and begins
climbing up the steep valley slopes. Eastwards it passes through some fields and
then bridges the river, heading towards the house on the north bank.
name: Bottom of a Steep Path

desc:
Three paths meet here, just south of the bridge. The main trail travels north
and west from here, while a smaller and less well-used one climbs up the valley
slopes and through some trees. On the other side of the Bruinen a large house
can be seen, overlooking the river from atop the steep bank.
name: Meeting of the Paths

desc:
You are on a path winding across the fairly steep slopes in the northwest part
of this valley. Below this point you can see the trail descend into a pine
forest, and later emerge from it nearer the river that runs through the centre
of the valley. To the west the path twists further as it seeks a way out of the
valley.
name: Twisting Path above a Pine Forest

desc:
This path is near the top of the valley walls, many hundreds of feet above the
valley floor down below. The path has been worn by many footprints over the
ages, and has become rather narrow and treacherous in parts. Although further to
the east is not such a poor path, near the top of the valley walls certainly is.
The final section requires any traveller to be extremely sure of foot.
name: Steep and Twisting Path

desc:
You are in the middle of a light pine forest, on a winding path. These trees
have obviously stood for hundreds of years, as their weight and girth will
attend to. Out to the east you can see the trail leave the forest and meet with
several other trails near a large house. Further up the trail becomes steeper
and leaves the tree-line behind.
name: Path Winding through a Pine Forest

desc:
You are on a path overlooking a pine forest, in a sheltered valley. Westwards it
leads across the slope towards the high moors above, while below it descends
into the forest. It becomes extremely steep and difficult there, as the trail is
little more than a stairway of tree roots and branches. You would have to be
very careful with your footing to climb down that way.
name: Track on the Outskirts of a Pine Forest

desc:
You feel a sense of comfort and well-being as you stroll along the paved
courtyard. You notice the upper floors are built on top of this plaza, except in
the centre where the sunlight warms this place by day, and the stars shine in by
night. The courtyard as a whole is a popular meeting place for the residents of
Rivendell, and you can see elves everywhere talking merrily or singing together.
You see a magnificent fountain to the north- east and some shops along the
perimeter of the yard.
name: The Courtyard

desc:
You feel a sense of comfort and well-being as you stroll along the paved
courtyard. You notice the upper floors are built on top of this plaza, except in
the centre where the sunlight warms this place by day, and the stars shine in by
night. The courtyard as a whole is a popular meeting place for the residents of
Rivendell, and you can see elves everywhere talking merrily or singing together.
You see a magnificent fountain to the north and some shops along the perimeter
of the yard.
name: The Courtyard

desc:
Here seems to be the central meeting place for the inhabitants of Rivendell. The
mood is so jubilant that you can't help but smile broadly as you wait here.
Unlike the rest of the courtyard, there is no roof above your head, allowing the
sunlight to shine in by day and the stars to be visible by night. Before you
stands a huge marble fountain with an awe-inspiring copper statue of Ulmo, the
Vala of the Waters. In the centre of the fountain lies a clear crystal, filling
the air with a soft radiance.
name: The Fountain Square

desc:
You feel a sense of comfort and well-being as you stroll along the paved
courtyard. You notice the upper floors are built on top of this plaza, except in
the centre where the sunlight warms this place by day, and the stars shine in by
night. The courtyard as a whole is a popular meeting place for the residents of
Rivendell, and you can see elves everywhere talking merrily or singing together.
You see a magnificent fountain to the east.
name: The Courtyard

desc:
You feel a sense of comfort and well-being as you stroll along the paved
courtyard. You notice the upper floors are built on top of this plaza, except in
the centre where the sunlight warms this place by day, and the stars shine in by
night. The courtyard as a whole is a popular meeting place for the residents of
Rivendell, and you can see elves everywhere talking merrily or singing together.
You see a magnificent fountain to the south- west and some shops along the
perimeter of the yard.
name: The Courtyard

desc:
The back of this spacious room is dominated by four large worktables, upon which
a variety of garments can be seen, each at a different stage of its creation. On
the table to the left, huge bolts of fabric lie next to sharp shears, used to
cut the material, whilst on the table furthest to the right, the clothing lies,
neatly folded, awaiting only finishing touches. On the two remaining tables, the
partially crafted garments lie, and at the front of the shop, the finished goods
are displayed on shelves and pegs for your perusal. A large sign has been hung
on the wall, beside the doorway, and a smaller piece of paper has been nailed
beside it.
name: The Rivendell Tailor Shop

desc:
This huge room is dug partially underground to regulate the temperature produced
by the many furnaces constantly in operation here. You can find smiths at work
at almost any hour, producing high quality armour and weapons. A sign stands
near the entrance.
name: The Rivendell Smithy

desc:
You feel a sense of comfort and well-being as you stroll along the paved
courtyard. You notice the upper floors are built on top of this plaza, except in
the centre where the sunlight warms this place by day, and the stars shine in by
night. The courtyard as a whole is a popular meeting place for the residents of
Rivendell, and you can see elves everywhere talking merrily or singing together.
You see a magnificent fountain to the north- west and some shops along the
perimeter of the yard.
name: The Courtyard

desc:
You feel a sense of comfort and well-being as you stroll along the paved
courtyard. You notice the upper floors are built on top of this plaza, except in
the centre where the sunlight warms this place by day, and the stars shine in by
night. The courtyard as a whole is a popular meeting place for the residents of
Rivendell, and you can see elves everywhere talking merrily or singing together.
You see a magnificent fountain to the west.
name: The Courtyard

desc:
Here the inhabitants of Rivendell come to informally honour the Ainur and Eru
Iluvatar. Especially dear to the elves is Varda, and for her the roof is opened
on clear nights to allow the starlight in. Important messages from the Ainur, as
interpreted by the Wise, are collected here for the enlightenment of all.
name: A Modest Temple

desc:
On a steep slope near the valley ridge, the elves have constructed a tiered,
irrigated, vineyard here. You see rows and rows of vines with big delicious red
grapes growing on them. Occasionally some workers will show up and take a bushel
of grapes to the House. To the north, a short walkway leads to a cottage.
name: Vineyards

desc:
These fertile fields draw irrigation from the river south of here and support a
range of crops. The Misty Mountains appear in breathtaking majesty to the east,
over the roof of a small house. To the north is the entrance to a small house.
name: Fields

desc:
The pleasant warmth of a bright fireplace welcomes all friendly visitors to the
house of the Elven wizard, explorer and part-time orc hunter Galawon. A large
wooden table, surrounded by chairs, stands in the middle of the room, upon a
brightly coloured carpet. In the southwestern corner a sofa and a couple of
armchairs offer a more comfortable way to rest. A mantelpiece, definitely
unusual in its nature, stands beside the front door, on the southern side of the
room; to the north, behind an arched passage, a smaller room can be seen. Wide
windows on the eastern wall offer a staggering view of the Misty Mountains.
name: Galawon's Living Room

desc:
The river passes over a small waterfall just downstream of here, and following
it the path climbs down on a stairway that has been cut from the rock. Upriver
the path hugs the cliff walls, and in parts becomes treacherously narrow. To the
west the gorge widens, and becomes a lush valley.
name: A Rocky Ledge by the River

desc:
This narrow river track winds around outcrops in the rock, following the trail
of the river. In parts it is not a path at all, merely rocks it is possible to
walk on. To continue the path someone has cut a steep stairway in the cliff
sides, which ascends into the gorge to the east. The river itself passes over a
small fall south of here and winds on down the valley.
name: Path by the River Bruinen

desc:
The Bruinen enters the vale of Rivendell in a great cataract, creating a
thundering roar and a great cloud of mist, and then continues down in a series
of cascades. At last the water plunges in a twenty-foot sheer arc, raising a
gentle mist. Downstream you can see it wind through the valley, pass through
some rapids and leave it as the cliff walls narrow again. Swiftly flowing as
most mountain streams usually are, this one appears to lose some of its speed to
the west of here, where it slows to a lethargic pace.
name: Bottom of a Waterfall in the Bruinen

desc:
Gleaming in the light of several torches, sharp and shining, are some of the
finest swords ever created. Fastened to the plain marble walls, as memories of
the past creations, the swords have been forged by the hands of Feanor, the
greatest of all the elven smiths, and his son Curufin, and in turn those of his
son, Celebrimbor the ring-maker. Not much else can be seen in this hall save the
bright light that beckons you from the south.
name: Hall of Swords

desc:
The light in this room comes from the walls themselves. Several gems, rubies,
emeralds, opals and sapphires have been inlaid to create a pattern of light that
beckons and calls, telling tales of the forgotten eras. The crystals on the
walls almost seem in motion, always giving a new pattern to follow, a new story
to learn. As the dazzling patterns and colours continue to swirl around you and
in your mind you snap back to reality with a sense of loss, but still, a sense
of joy and love.
name: Hall of Crystal

desc:
These fertile fields draw irrigation from the river south of here and support a
range of crops. The Misty Mountains appear in breathtaking majesty to the east.
You can enter the river here via the irrigation canals.
name: Fields

desc:
These fertile fields draw irrigation from the river south of here and support a
range of crops. The Misty Mountains appear in breathtaking majesty to the east.
You can enter the river here via the irrigation canals.
name: Fields

desc:
In a single curving span this bridge leaps twenty feet over the Bruinen, without
rail or kerb. At first it would appear to be less than safe, but underfoot the
off-white stone of which it is made proves to be of exceptional grip. Like all
things of Elven manufacture it fits beautifully with nature - its simple and
minimal profile does not blot the landscape, instead enhancing it with its own
grace.
name: Gracefully Arched Bridge

desc:
Several trails meet here, in the centre of the northern river bank. Paths to the
north and west lead into the large pine forest that dominates this side of the
valley, while to the south another trail bridges the river. To the east a large
house sits atop the steep bank, surrounded by gardens and some smaller buildings
- perhaps this is the fabled Last Homely House of Elrond Half-Elven.
name: Meeting of the Trails

desc:
A beautifully painted archway, with a silver plaque set above it, gives access
to the House of Elrond. North of here a large field can be seen, while to the
east the sound of singing and merriment can be heard in the courtyard. To the
south you notice the Ranger's House, where the noble rangers are quartered.
name: Entrance to the Last Homely Home

desc:
The Rivendell Jeweller has been recently restored and expanded, still containing
the largest wealth of gems in all of Eriador, but now stocking smaller pieces of
jewellery. Behind a finely carved desk stands a tall Elf, and in front of him
the largest range of priceless items you might ever see; their value maybe
matching that of the Arkenstone itself. A sign rests on the counter.
name: Jeweller

desc:
On one wall, a window allows sight of the outside. Otherwise, this room is
unremarkable. Your eye sees boxes and bags filled with herbs and dried plants.
Spices seem to be this store's trade.
name: Herbalist

desc:
The furnishings provided are nothing spectacular, just some comfortable beds
against the west and east walls with a simple chest at the foot of each. No
possessions are left here, and the room is usually empty, as most rangers spend
extended periods in the wilderness far away from this sheltered vale. However,
some of the most skilled ones work in shifts to provide around-the- clock
training for young Dunedain and elves eager to learn. These instructors stress
leadership skills and self-confidence, and this shows through in the excellence
of the ranger organization.
name: Ranger's Quarters

desc:
Guests of Elrond leave their horses and pets here in the care of professional
stablehands where they are routinely fed, groomed, and exercised while their
owners are enjoying a rest in Rivendell. In addition, fresh horses are always
kept available for those in need of transport.
name: The Rivendell Stables

desc:
At this simple stand a cheerful elf will arrange a pet for you from Rivendell's
stock of excellent trained animals. There is a sign posted on the wall.
name: Pet Stand

desc:
The path winds through the pine forest, getting steeper the further up you look.
There it gets so difficult that you would need to scramble up the slope, using
the roots and branches as steps. Another path branches off to the north, where
it suddenly levels out as it begins to travel along the slope towards the east.
The trees themselves are gnarled and ancient, obviously having overlooked this
valley for many hundreds of years. A low stone building stands east of here.
name: Trail through a Pine Forest

desc:
This short path leads east to the agricultural fields that feed Rivendell. To
the west there is a small archway that leads to the courtyard of the Last Homely
House.
name: Path to the Fields

desc:
One of the many paths from the moors nears the cliff edge, and descends several
hundred feet across the slope. The valley itself is an oasis of green in this
bleak landscape, shielded far below from the harsh winds that whistle across the
moors. It is obviously inhabited, as a large house and some fields can be seen
below.
name: At the Top of a Valley

desc:
Several large windows open up this room to the stars and the sun, letting the
splendour and light of the West fill and caress the features of the room. The
light is reflected in a myriad of colours from crystals inlaid in carvings and
statues. Pillars of marble lift the dome-shaped ceiling shaped as to create a
feeling of space and freedom and two small trees grow in the centre of the room
act as a constant reminder of the lost Trees, Telperion and Laurelin and thus
the light of the Silmarilli, the great gems that Maglor fought so many battles
for.
name: The Domed Chamber

desc:
This medium-sized room looks like a mixture between a library, a laboratory and
a geographer's workshop. Heavy tomes are aligned on many shelves fixed to the
walls; some other ancient-looking books lie in glass boxes, to preserve them
from exposure. A wooden desk lies in front of the windows, to the east; some
more texts are piled in a corner, while great part of the desk is taken by a
spread map of the Misty Mountains east of Rivendell. Many quill pens and vials
of ink rest in an open drawer, always ready for use. Other maps hang from the
walls, showing places both near and far. In the northwestern corner there is
another table, this time made of marble; strangely coloured patches mar its
otherwise perfect surface. Doorways lead both south, to the living room, and
west, to Galawon's bedroom.
name: Galawon's Study

desc:
This bedroom's windows, as opposed to the ones in the rest of the house, face
west, towards the house of Elrond, and north. A comfortable bed lies in the
southwestern corner; a finely carved oak nightstand is beside it. A few
well-thumbed books rest on the nightstand, probably Galawon uses them to read
before sleeping. Shown on display on the walls you can see Galawon's most
treasured trophies, a dented two-handed sword and a shredded fragment of black
cloth, together with fine oil paintings of various landscapes of Arda. The only
door is east, to Galawon's study.
name: Galawon's Private Quarters

desc:
A large pavilion has been hastily but efficiently erected here in a clearing for
the shelter of elves who have vacated their homes in the wilderness. The walls
are silken cloth; the area is roofed with withes cemented with clay. Comfortable
pallets are arranged here; bundles and crates are stacked nearby, containing the
possessions of the refugees.
name: A Temporary Shelter

desc:
The river enters some rapids here, making swimming much harder. Flecks of foam
and spray spit into the air, obscuring your vision, and the current begins to
tug you westward with increasing speed. Looking downstream, where the valley
narrows to a gorge, you can see the river being churned between towering walls
of stone. To the east the river is calmer, and is crossed in a single span by a
slender bridge.
name: Small Rapids in the River Bruinen

desc:
This modest suite has walls crafted of oak matching the tall oak tree that grows
nearby. Giant arched windows have been carefully carved out of the oak wood. In
the mornings the sun flows through them illuminating the room. A grouping of
shelves are lined with jewels and ancient artefacts. On the ceiling there is a
beautiful, embellished carving of an Elven sorceress and her Knight, they seem
to stare down on the room with lifelike eyes.
name: Carlton's Suite

desc:
This small hall extends off the River House and serves as passage to some select
private quarters. The walls are made of oak and lined with white benches
ornately carved from aged birch trees. Large wooden planters filled with
fragrant flowers and herbs sit on either side of each bench.
name: A Short Hall

desc:
The natural wood architecture of this room conveys the peaceful aura of an elven
forest. A set of large carved windows in the southern wall open up to a stunning
view of the River Bruinen as it flows through the valley below. Looking
upstream, one can see a modest pier and turning one's head just a bit more the
patio of the River House is clearly visible.
name: Tryalno's Sanctuary

desc:
A vast lawn of green grass grows all around, providing food for the many horses
you see prancing about. Other horses are being ridden or led about by trainers.
In one area, there are some dogs being commanded to perform basic actions, and
some falcons and eagles are circling overhead, occasionally heading down to the
ground. In another area archery targets and length markers have been erected. To
the east lies a large wooden stable, and west is a stand where fine trained
animals are sold. To the north is the warrior's yard, where the elves train to
fight the creatures of Sauron.
name: A Large Field

desc:
Young students, both men and elves, are trained here in the style of the elven
warrior, which emphasizes swift attacks and avoidance of blows. There are always
groups here, sparring or learning new techniques. Just south of here is a large
field, where horsemanship and jousting can be mastered.
name: Warrior Training Yard

desc:
This room is quite crowded with furniture, dozens of shelves filled with books,
bandages and herbs and potions covering the walls. Almost hidden among all this
is a picture on the eastern wall of Rilmamir's hero, a strange looking man,
wielding a brutal cleaver. While the floor itself is spotless, a large number of
used bandages and half-drunk potions lay scattered about showing this room is
often used by wounded warriors Rilmamir must have treated. A smell of dried,
exotic flowers and athelas float in the air, covering the smell of blood.
name: Rilmamir's House of Healing

desc:
The walls are clad in tapestries, telling stories about Durin's folk and how
they conquered Khazad-dum but finally got defeated themselves, about battles of
ancient times and about treasures lost forever. The well crafted hallway leads
to a council chamber north of here, and ends in a sturdy stone door south of
here.
name: Entrance Hall

desc:
A small, warm chamber without any decorations. At the southern wall a small bed
is placed, and the walls are hung with blue and purple tapestries.
name: Nain's Chamber

desc:
Along the path to the fields, travellers often find this humble garden awaits
them inviting those who pass near to sit down and relax. Often times a gentle
breeze passes through causing the flowers and grass sway softly. The sound of
birds singing quitely high in the trees and fluttering of butterflies and
buzzing of bees are also common sights and sounds as they fly among the Alfirn.
The whole place seems to have been designed to create a soothing ambience. This
garden is clearly filled with the magic of its elven caretakers.
name: Earile's Garden of Meditation

desc:
This is a nature-like hallway decorated with purple and yellow flowers. At the
end of the hallway there is a small opening leading into a square, which seems
to reflect all the colours of the rainbow. You also notice a big sign nailed to
the northern door made of oak.
name: The Vestibule

desc:
You are in a garden made of crystal. If you look long enough at the crystals you
can see all the colours play softly; for some, this gives the sensation of peace
and righteousness. There is also a cosy bed made of leaves for meditation and
rest among the crystals.
name: Crystal Garden

desc:
The walls of this chamber look old and grey. There is a huge bed placed against
the western wall and a fresh scent of baked bread fills the air. You also notice
a table and a chair placed on the other side of the chamber.
name: Chamber of Eternal Sleep

desc:
As you pass through the mist a large cavern is revealed. The walls are smooth
and flat from being worn away by the elements for countless years. As you walk
further into the room it grows dark, the sounds of the roaring waterfall seem to
be left behind you. Further ahead you see lights illuminating a staircase
descending into the depths.
name: Large Smooth Cavern

desc:
The staircase is lit with torches burning along the walls which provide enough
light to proceed. As you continue further down the steps you notice that the
staircase was carved into the rock. The steps twist and turn further down into
the earth. You see a faint glow around the bend.
name: Descending Staircase

desc:
As you finally reach the end of the staircase you walk into a decorated chamber
fit for a king. A large bed rests along the far wall accompanied by a small
table where a candle burns. Around the chamber you see large chests filled with
jewels and gold casting a faint glow upon the walls.
name: Jerle's Chamber

desc:
Right after passing the waterfall, begins a flowing water path surrounded by
trees of the most various types. The air is thick and short rays of light can
barely be seen penetrating through the dense foliage of the highest trees.
Following the path, some soft and light music seems to float along through the
vegetation, carried by the smooth wind that blows from the south end of the path
towards the north. To the south, the line of plants growing in the border of the
path leads inside a home; without a doubt, the home of a wood elf.
name: Watery Path among the Trees

desc:
Perfectly carved branches of wood make the walls of this hall. Comfortable
furniture, made of the finest oak wood, like only elves could build, seems very
inviting for a rest around a large fireplace. If it wasn't for the sound of the
rushing water of the Bruinen, one could imagine being inside a camp house of a
wood elf in the middle of the wilderness. Everything is kept perfectly
organised, most of the things looking untouched. The master of this house is
surely a good traveller, who comes here between one adventure or another to
rest, and maybe meet his friends for everlasting nights of stories. Twisted
wooden stairs lead upward into a calm room.
name: The Oak Hall

desc:
Eolo's house is incredibly comfortable, the way it has to be for someone who
comes home exclusively to rest. His bed, large and smooth, stays in the corner
of the room, which has the floor covered with a large green carpet of leaves. A
large tree is carved out and full of warm water, ready for someone to take a
long and relaxing bath. A strong wind flows through the large window, when it is
left open for fresh air. Keeping the window closed, the tree house remains quiet
enough for the sound of a beetle climbing up the walls to be heard.
name: Eolo's Tree House

desc:
A burrow has been dug here through the brown, sandy soil. Underfoot the soil is
compacted and dense; it has been worn down to partially expose a dense gravel
layer underneath. To the south the soil on the floor of the tunnel gives way
entirely to gravel, then larger chunks of rock. Most of the larger rocks have
been piled against the walls, narrowing the tunnel and creating a bottleneck.
name: Crude Tunnel

desc:
The trolls have expended great effort in creating this tunnel; sandy, grey-brown
dirt and large blocks of granite have been cut straight through as the tunnel
continues its path deep underground. Holes in the walls and floor show where
boulders were removed during the digging process; the resulting cavities have
been left unfilled, lending an uneven look to the walls and creating hazardous
pits in the ground. The crudely-dug tunnel branches here, leading off in three
separate directions.
name: Crude Tunnel

desc:
The water in this subterranean basin is cloudy with dirt and unidentifiable
particles of organic matter. A layer of sediment is visible on the bottom of the
basin; when the water is disturbed, the sediment rises to cloud the water and
reduce visibility. The rock overhead is grey and dark; it forms a smooth
expanse, unbroken by crevices or pockets that could trap air. The basin widens
slightly towards the north, and narrows to the south.
name: Underground Basin

desc:
An uneven, rugged tunnel has been cut through the hard, grey, granite bedrock.
Large chunks of rock protrude from the ground, pebbles litter the slope, and
loose rocks lie where their downhill roll was stopped by the aforementioned
obstacles. Further down the tunnel, the bottom of the passageway is visible.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel has been dug through the coarse-grained granite bedrock, and
tremendous effort and determination must have been required to tunnel through
such dense, hard stone. The cavern continues to the west and east. To the east
it slopes down steeply. The rock surface of the slope is covered with furrows
and gouges; it looks as if the rocks broken loose during the digging were simply
dumped into the tunnel and down the slope.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
Water has gathered in a natural basin in the southern part of this cavern. The
dark waters reveal nothing of the depth of the subterranean lake. The
surrounding walls are wet with condensation; growths of fungi and moss thrive
here. The cavern may have been isolated once, but the eastern wall has been dug
through and a low passage leads into a tunnel.
name: Edge of an Underground Basin

desc:
This deep, narrow passage has filled with water. A sediment layer is visible on
the bottom of the basin, and is stirred up when the water is disturbed. The
walls and bottom are of solid rock, and the sharp edges of protruding stones
clearly show that this is a body of still water, because flowing water would
have smoothed the edges over time. The pool is not deep here, and the
reflections from the surface above are clearly visible.
name: Bottom of a Basin

desc:
The passageway here, a tunnel through bedrock of coarse grey granite, has
collapsed with devastating results. The floor disappears into a jagged hole with
the bottom barely visible below. On the far side of the hole the continuing
tunnel is forever sealed by enormous masses of fallen rubble. The tunnel
continues to the east: following it in that direction may be safer than
attempting to climb down into the hole.
name: Dangerous Cave-in

desc:
This soft-walled tunnel, comprised of a curious reddish-brown soil, comes to a
dead end here. The soil underfoot at the end of the tunnel is very densely
packed; above, a large, irregularly-shaped hole can be seen. The air stirs at
irregular intervals as drafts blow down from the hole. However, as the hole is
too high to reach, the only passable exit is to the west.
name: Tunnel of Blood-red Dirt

desc:
You feel a sense of comfort and well-being as you stroll along the paved
courtyard. You notice the upper floors are built on top of this plaza, except in
the centre where the sunlight warms this place by day, and the stars shine in by
night. The courtyard as a whole is a popular meeting place for the residents of
Rivendell, and you can see elves everywhere talking merrily or singing together.
You see a magnificent fountain to the south and some shops along the perimeter
of the yard.
name: The Courtyard

desc:
You feel a sense of comfort and well-being as you stroll along the paved
courtyard. You notice the upper floors are built on top of this plaza, except in
the centre where the sunlight warms this place by day, and the stars shine in by
night. The courtyard as a whole is a popular meeting place for the residents of
Rivendell, and you can see elves everywhere talking merrily or singing together.
You see a magnificent fountain to the south- east and some shops along the
perimeter of the yard.
name: The Courtyard

desc:
Many guards and citizens traffic through this office. It is here that the
cityguards' agenda is planned and executed to keep the peace in Rivendell. On
the wall you see some pictures of known criminals you should keep an eye out
for, and report them to the guards. A notice hangs on the wall behind the
secretary's desk.
name: Guards' Office

desc:
A flat-file shelving unit carved out of white wood and bearing Elven markings
labelling each shelf stands here against the south wall serving as a home for
countless rolled parchments. In the middle of the room is a large table that
fills up most of this small office. Several high-back chairs with deep green
cloth cushions surround the table, which is covered with maps.
name: Deeds, Writs and Maps

desc:
The room is small and cosy. The walls are covered with bookshelves packed with
ancient books and scrolls. The room is poorly lit with only a small, round
window that is high up on the wall, opposite to the door. The curved ceiling
makes the room appear very old. The aura of this room is calm and relaxing,
creating a focused environment.
name: Magi's Temple

desc:
Those entering this room would probably only see a few simple things at first
glimpse. A table, a bed, and a chair are most prominent. Further investigation
would reveal, several drawings, maps, and an array of weapons adorning the walls
of this dimly lit lodge. Here, scouts often gather from all over, to discuss
movements, opinions, and plans for dealing with Sauron's minions.
name: Paisa's Lodge

desc:
Black-painted stone was used along the base for a foundation of this workshop,
with a silver, decorative moulding that is waist-high, to separate the stone
from the rest of the white and glossy walls that travel to a high and vaulted
ceiling. A small stove made of cast iron stands along the south wall, vented up
through the ceiling where it disappears from sight; two porcelain crucibles that
are slender and easy to work are on top of it. A door and handle line the front
of the stove were a small fire burns even now. A shelf stands in the corner with
metal castings that appear silver and very fine, all in an orderly stack next to
a cast iron mould for molten metal; the mould is used to make twelve arrowheads
at a time of various sizes. Next to the table is a clay pot, almost spilling at
the rim with all of the arrowheads made here; the grey pot contrasts off the
black wall behind it. A corner rack is filled with many bows of various sizes
and design, with arrows on display as well.
name: Jaagern's Foundry

desc:
The masters at this school teach the arts of stealth to those who would oppose
the forces of darkness. In better times, such skills were used to hunt game for
the many elven celebrations. The revelry is now a thing of the past, and the
hunters' quarry changed. More often than not, the hunters become the prey. The
guilds skills are much in demand even by the most righteous of warriors. A path
in the north opens between two large bushes.
name: Hunter's Guild

desc:
Glossy white trim with an intricate design carved into it runs along the top of
this room where the walls meet the ceiling, with traces of silver embedded
throughout the wood. The walls are painted a light colour grey and appearing
swirled to add some texture. Several small and silver sconces decorate the
walls, each sconce holding a slender white candle that was left burning. Bows of
an experienced huntress are suspended on the western wall, with easy access if
needed for battle or war, close to the door. Below the bows is a tall and
slender vase that is black as night, filled with a variety of arrows. Along the
east side of the room is a bed and small table next to it, with several daggers
on display upon black pegs that are fastened to the wall.
name: Risneth's Chamber
